coverline: It bugs me that developers don't follow the standards...

page.title: The Fixer
page.description: Technical depth and human judgment applied together: frontend architecture, component systems, and engineering quality, framed through problem framing, trade-offs, communication, and delivery.

hero.title: The Fixer
hero.lead: Technical depth. Human judgment. Applied together.
hero.intro: I bring a broad frontend engineering foundation, but the stronger signal is how I apply it: framing ambiguous problems, selecting the right intervention, aligning people around trade-offs, and carrying decisions into reliable product delivery.

labels.core: Technical depth and human judgment: the core signal, hard skills, soft skills, and those capabilities in combination
labels.impact: The impact of applying technical depth through judgment, communication, and delivery

items: Connects frontend depth with product and system context.
Separates symptoms from root causes before implementation.
Makes constraints, risks, and trade-offs visible.
Combines technical direction with communication, alignment, and delivery.

primaryContent.title: Hard Skills
paragraphs: My technical range is centered on the web platform and the systems built above it.

items.title: Frontend Architecture
items.description: Boundaries, state, data flow, API integration, caching, validation, and error handling.

items.title: Component Systems
items.description: Reusable APIs, composition, tokens, Web Components, accessibility, and documentation.

items.title: Engineering Quality
items.description: Testing, performance, code standards, maintainability, and prevention.

items.title: Technical Foundation
items.description: Semantic HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, Angular, React, Node.js, REST, and GraphQL.

diagram.title: Technical Capability Stack
diagram.description: Shows how foundational web expertise supports increasingly broader engineering responsibilities.
diagram.caption: My technical range grows from durable web foundations rather than isolated framework expertise.
diagram.summary: A progression from Web Standards and Accessibility, to Component Systems, to Frontend Architecture, to Product Architecture, to AI-Enabled Product Capabilities.

primaryContent.title: Soft Skills
paragraphs: Staff-level execution depends on more than identifying the technically correct answer.

items.title: Problem Framing
items.description: Turning incomplete signals into a problem the team can evaluate.

items.title: Technical Judgment
items.description: Balancing quality, delivery pressure, risk, and long-term direction.

items.title: Communication
items.description: Making decisions understandable across engineering, product, and design.

items.title: Leadership
items.description: Providing technical direction, mentoring engineers, coordinating work, and building alignment without relying on authority alone.

primaryContent.title: Skills in Combination
paragraphs: These capabilities are most useful together. A component migration can require architectural boundaries, accessibility, test strategy, rollout sequencing, product negotiation, and team guidance—not simply implementation.
I move between implementation detail and system context so the immediate problem can be resolved without weakening the product around it.

diagram.title: Staff Execution Loop
diagram.description: Shows how technical and interpersonal capabilities move a problem from uncertainty to sustainable delivery.
diagram.caption: Hard skills determine what is possible; soft skills turn the decision into shared execution.
diagram.summary: A loop: Ambiguity, then Diagnosis, then Trade-Off, then Alignment, then Delivery, then Feedback, which informs the next problem.

items.title: Clearer Decisions
items.description: Technical options are evaluated through explicit constraints, risks, and consequences.

items.title: Stronger Quality
items.description: Accessibility, testing, performance, and maintainability are treated as part of engineering—not final checks.

items.title: Cross-Team Leverage
items.description: Clear communication helps product, design, and engineering act on the same technical model.

items.title: Safer Product Evolution
items.description: Immediate delivery remains connected to stronger boundaries, reusable patterns, and future change.

items.title: The Server
items.description: Provides the broader operating model behind how I think, collaborate, and take responsibility.

items.title: The Integrator
items.description: Shows how these capabilities connect systems, teams, product needs, and execution at Staff scope.

items.title: The Assembly
items.description: Examines the component contracts, composition, testing, and accessibility behind my frontend work.

items.title: Thomson Reuters
items.description: Provides current evidence of applying technical depth and leadership within established product and platform constraints.

closingThought.statement: The strongest skill is not knowing every tool; it is knowing which capability, decision, and collaboration pattern the problem requires.
